name: BROADRIDGE FINANCIAL SOLUTIONS, INC.
business: Broadridge is a global financial technology leader providing investor communications and technology-driven solutions to financial institutions. The company operates in two segments: Investor Communication Solutions (ICS) and Global Technology and Operations (GTO). Its services include proxy processing, regulatory communications, securities processing, and wealth management technology.

exposures.sentence: We have introduced a number of AI-driven solutions such as BondGPT and OpsGPT with agentic capabilities while continuing to develop new AI applications to create a more efficient trading process for our clients.

Our blockchain-enabled Distributed Ledger Repo (DLR) platform combines distributed ledger technology with existing market settlement infrastructure to provide clients added flexibility to manage their liquidity needs and execute cross-border intraday repo transactions through our DLR network.
We face ongoing cybersecurity threats to our information technology infrastructure including data loss, data exfiltration, denial of service, and ransomware, among others.

We support financial services, healthcare, insurance, consumer finance, telecommunications, utilities, and other service industries with omni-channel customer communications management strategies for their transactional communications, including statements and bills, marketing communications, such as personalized microsites and campaigns, and regulatory communications, such as trade confirmations and explanations of benefits.

catalysts.event: Proposed acquisition of Acolin Group Holdco Limited expected to close
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, Broadridge announced the proposed acquisition of Acolin Group Holdco Limited (Acolin). The acquisition is expected to close in the first half of Broadridge’s 2026 fiscal year.

catalysts.event: Completion of restructuring actions including facility closure
catalysts.sentence: The total estimated pre-tax costs for actions and associated costs related to the closure are approximately $20 million to $25 million and are expected to be completed by the end of the second quarter of fiscal year 2026.
